critical_invariants: TSC calibration value must remain accurate - incorrect calibration breaks all timing
Timer frequency must match configured APIC timer divisor
APIC timer LVT entry format is hardware-defined
RDTSC instruction pair (RDTSC + shl + or) for full 64-bit timestamp must remain intact
Timer interrupt must increment system tick counter atomically
PIT (8254) frequency for calibration: 1.193182 MHz is hardware-fixed
b_delay must not return early - minimum delay guarantee must be preserved
known_opportunities: RDTSC optimization: use RDTSCP for serialized timestamp reads (avoids LFENCE+RDTSC)
Busy-wait to PAUSE: insert PAUSE in delay spin loops to reduce power and pipeline contention
TSC deadline mode: use IA32_TSC_DEADLINE MSR for precise one-shot timer instead of periodic
Constant-TSC detection: skip calibration on CPUs with invariant TSC (CPUID.80000007H:EDX[8])
Timer coalescing: batch multiple pending timer events into single handler invocation
MWAIT-based delay for long waits to reduce CPU power consumption
Avoid redundant RDTSC calls by caching recent timestamp within same handler
